description: Reference strain used as the baseline control for experiments.
dnaRepairCharacteristics: All major DNA repair pathways intact
Functional homologous recombination
Functional non-homologous end joining
Normal DNA damage checkpoint responses
Normal chromosome segregation machinery
expectedPhenotypes: Normal growth under standard laboratory conditions
Used as comparison for mutant strains

dnaRepairCharacteristics: Severe impairment of homologous recombination
Defective double-strand break repair through RAD52-mediated pathways
Reduced gene conversion events
Reduced break-induced repair efficiency
Greater reliance on alternative repair mechanisms

description: Shugoshin-deficient strain affecting chromosome segregation and centromere function.
dnaRepairCharacteristics: DNA repair pathways largely intact
Defects primarily associated with centromere and chromosome segregation functions
Altered pericentromeric organization
May influence repair outcomes near centromeres indirectly
expectedPhenotypes: Potential effects on dicentric chromosome behavior

description: Spindle-associated mutant involved in chromosome segregation and mitotic progression.
dnaRepairCharacteristics: No major core DNA repair pathway deletion
Primarily affects spindle dynamics and chromosome behavior during mitosis
Can alter chromosome stability indirectly
expectedPhenotypes: Potential effects on dicentric chromosome resolution

description: Triple nuclease mutant affecting multiple DNA structure processing pathways.
dnaRepairCharacteristics: Loss of MUS81 structure-selective endonuclease activity
Loss of YEN1 Holliday junction resolution activity
Loss of RAD1 nuclease function involved in DNA repair processing
Defective processing of recombination intermediates
Reduced ability to resolve complex DNA repair structures
expectedPhenotypes: Reduced resolution of Holliday junction-like structures
Altered repair outcomes following chromosome breakage
